2026-05-29.patch_summary: This patch fixes an issue where the userfaultfd VMA flags bitmap was being written out of bounds on 32-bit architectures. The problem occurred because the bit numbers used in __VMA_UFFD_FLAGS were not valid for all architectures, leading to silent corruption of the mask. To fix this, the patch introduces a new function mk_vma_flags_from_masks() that builds the mask from whole per-mode masks that collapse to EMPTY_VMA_FLAGS when their feature is unavailable. This approach ensures that out-of-range bits are never materialized and the in-range fast path remains a compile-time constant.

reviews.author: Lorenzo Stoakes
reviews.summary:
The reviewer suggested using a different commit (a06eb2f8279e) to modify __VMA_UFFD_FLAGS, citing it as the original source of this flag.

Gave Reviewed-by
reviews.raw_body:
Hmm, I think commit a06eb2f8279e ("mm/vma: convert
vma_modify_flags[_uffd]() to use vma_flags_t") is more appropriate isn't
it?

As that's the commit that added __VMA_UFFD_FLAGS :)

2026-06-01.patch_summary: This patch modifies the userfaultfd feature in the Linux kernel to build __VMA_UFFD_FLAGS from config-gated masks instead of feeding bit numbers that may not exist on the current build to mk_vma_flags(). This change prevents out-of-bounds writes and ensures that the mask is correct regardless of the architecture. The patch introduces new macros VMA_UFFD_MISSING, VMA_UFFD_WP, and VMA_UFFD_MINOR, which are defined based on the availability of the corresponding features. On 32-bit architectures, these macros collapse to EMPTY_VMA_FLAGS when their feature is unavailable.

reviews.author: Mike Rapoport
reviews.summary: The reviewer requested that the patch author ask Claude to improve the changelog's conciseness and paragraph structure.
reviews.raw_body:
Can you ask claude to produce more concise changelogs and better split it
to paragraphs?

Reviewed-by: Mike Rapoport (Microsoft) <rppt@kernel.org>

2026-06-02.patch_summary: This patch modifies the userfaultfd feature in the Linux kernel to build __VMA_UFFD_FLAGS from config-gated masks instead of feeding bit numbers that may not exist on the current build to mk_vma_flags(). This change prevents out-of-bounds writes and ensures that the mask is correct regardless of the architecture. The patch introduces new macros VMA_UFFD_MISSING, VMA_UFFD_WP, and VMA_UFFD_MINOR, which are defined based on the availability of the corresponding features. On 32-bit architectures, these macros collapse to EMPTY_VMA_FLAGS when their feature is unavailable.
